question: The scatter plot shows marketing spend vs. revenue. Which statement is most consistent with the data?

options.A: Higher marketing spend is clearly associated with lower revenue.
options.B: There is a generally positive relationship between marketing spend and revenue.
options.C: Marketing spend of around $80k–$100k generates far higher revenue than any other spend level, as suggested by the largest, darkest points.
options.D: There is no relationship at all between marketing spend and revenue.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
